steps.title: Find the pronoun’s antecedent and its number
steps.content:
The sentence opens with “Water in the North Atlantic Ocean” as the subject. The blank fills a pronoun that refers back to “Water” and must agree with it in number. “Water” is a singular, uncountable noun, so the pronoun must be singular.
  • Choice A — these: Plural demonstrative pronoun — does not agree with the singular “Water.” ✗
  • Choice B — those: Plural demonstrative pronoun — does not agree with the singular “Water.” ✗
  • Choice C — them: Plural object pronoun — does not agree with the singular “Water.” ✗
  • Choice D — it: Singular pronoun — agrees in number with “Water” and clearly refers to the water swirling into the gyre. ✓

steps.title: Confirm and conclude
steps.content: Every other choice is plural and conflicts with the singular antecedent “Water.” Only “it” satisfies pronoun-antecedent agreement. That confirms D is correct.
